title: alnus serrulata var. rugosa
intro: Alnus serrulata var. rugosa, also known as the rough-barked alder, is a variety of the American alder tree (Alnus serrulata) that is native to eastern North America. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bark is rich in tannins and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Alnus serrulata var. rugosa can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
